2606.16447.title: Training and Evaluating Diffusion Policies with Long Context Lengths
2606.16447.paper_summary: Imitation learning has enabled highly-dexterous robotic manipulation from RGB observations. Policies trained with these methods, however, typically condition robot actions on only a short history of observations. These policies cannot solve tasks that require memory and can get stuck repeatedly executing the same failing motions. In this work, we first benchmark policy performance as context length is incrementally increased from short to long, across a spectrum of tasks with varying local stability and memory requirements, and in multiple data regimes. To our knowledge, this is the first study to investigate context length in imitation learning at this level of detail. Our results challenge prior claims: naively scaling context length is not as brittle as advertised in literature. With an appropriate conditioning method and denoising backbone (UNet+Cross-Attention), single-task policies achieve high success rates on many tasks in the usual data regime even with naive scaling. Next, we propose a training algorithm to jointly train policies at multiple context lengths, further reducing the sample complexity of long-context learning. Finally, we apply our findings to re-evaluate some previously proposed solutions to long-context imitation learning.
2606.16447.paper_summary_zh: 模仿學習使得從 RGB 觀察中實現高靈活性的機器人操作成為可能。然而，使用這些方法訓練的策略通常僅根據短期觀察歷史來調節機器人行動。這些策略無法解決需要記憶的任務，並且可能會不斷重複執行相同的失敗動作。在本研究中，我們首先基準評估策略性能，隨著上下文長度從短到長逐步增加，涵蓋了多種具有不同局部穩定性和記憶需求的任務，以及多種數據範疇。據我們所知，這是第一項在這一細節層面上研究模仿學習中上下文長度的研究。我們的結果挑戰了先前的主張：簡單地擴展上下文長度並不像文獻中所宣稱的那樣脆弱。通過適當的調節方法和去噪骨幹（UNet+Cross-Attention），單任務策略在通常的數據範疇中，即使在簡單擴展的情況下，也能在許多任務上達到高成功率。接下來，我們提出了一種訓練算法，以聯合訓練多個上下文長度的策略，進一步降低長上下文學習的樣本複雜性。最後，我們將我們的發現應用於重新評估一些先前提出的長上下文模仿學習解決方案。
2606.16447.author: Abhinav Agarwal et.al.
2606.16447.authors: Abhinav Agarwal, Adam Wei, Taylan Kargin, Michael Zeng, Cole Becker, Arif Kerem Dayi, Pablo Parrilo, Asuman Ozdaglar, Russ Tedrake
